source_test: HEC.FDA.Statistics/Distributions/Empirical.cs StackEmpiricalDistributions + HEC.FDA.Statistics/Histograms/DynamicHistogram.cs ConvertToEmpiricalDistribution @ f63682a86a30dc306a105689714a92bfd95956c5 -- no dedicated upstream test file for either method exists, so `construct` is hand-specified: `distributions` cases build strictly-increasing (probability, value) Empirical inputs (matching the Empirical(double[] probabilities, double[] observationValues) ctor) with an explicit `sample_mean` set on each (SampleMean is a settable property Empirical's own ctors never assign), then dispatch through Empirical::stack_empirical_distributions with `op` sum/subtract; `histogram` cases build a DynamicHistogram the same way as fixtures/histograms/dynamic_histogram.json, then dispatch through DynamicHistogram::convert_to_empirical_distribution. Expected values are COMPUTE-THEN-PIN: computed by the C++ port, then confirmed reproduced by the real C# Empirical/DynamicHistogram via tools/oracle_emitter / tools/verify_oracles.py -- no value here is invented independently of either implementation.

assertions.note: combine(op=sum) applied left-to-right across each input's own SampleMean: 30.0 + 25.0
propagated from the source histogram's raw running SampleMean, not recomputed by Empirical's ctor
